statement.en: Attention-based multiple instance learning for predominant growth pattern prediction in lung adenocarcinoma wsi using foundation models
statement.summary: Lung adenocarcinoma (LUAD) grading depends on accurately identifying growth patterns, which are indicators of prognosis and can influence treatment decisions. Common deep learning approaches to determine the predominant pattern rely on patch-level classification or segmentation, requiring extensive annotations. This study proposes an attention-based multiple instance learning (ABMIL) framework to predict the predominant LUAD growth pattern at the whole slide level to reduce annotation burden. Our approach integrates pretrained pathology foundation models as patch encoders, used either frozen or fine-tuned on annotated patches, to extract discriminative features that are aggregated through attention mechanisms. Experiments show that fine-tuned encoders improve performance, with Prov-GigaPat

proposer: Laura Valeria Perez-Herrera, M. J. Garcia-Gonzalez, Karen Lopez-Linares

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: This is a preprint dated 2026-04-25 describing a novel computational method (ABMIL + foundation models for histopathology). The abstract is incomplete (cuts off at 'Prov-GigaPat'), preventing full evaluation of empirical validation status. The methodology is sound in principle but requires peer review confirmation of claims regarding performance improvements and generalization. As a 2026-dated preprint, validation status is provisional.
